questions.explanation: Ampulla is the widest part where fertilization typically occurs.

questions.questionText: The notochord induces the formation of the:
questions.explanation: Notochord signals the overlying ectoderm to form the neural plate.

questions.questionText: The first germ layer to form during gastrulation is:
questions.explanation: Epiblast cells displace hypoblast to form definitive endoderm first.

questions.questionText: Meckel's cartilage is a derivative of which pharyngeal arch?
questions.explanation: First arch cartilage forms malleus, incus, and sphenomandibular ligament.

questions.questionText: The thyroid gland develops mainly from the:
questions.explanation: An endodermal downgrowth from the foramen cecum forms the thyroid.

questions.questionText: Tetralogy of Fallot is a defect in the development of the:
questions.explanation: Anterior displacement of the conotruncal septum causes TOF.

questions.questionText: The paramesonephric (Mullerian) ducts in females develop into all EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Lower 1/3 of vagina develops from the urogenital sinus.

questions.questionText: Cleft lip is caused by failure of fusion of:
questions.explanation: Failure of fusion causes anterior cleft anomalies.

questions.questionText: The kidney (metanephros) begins to produce urine by:
questions.explanation: Urine production starts around weeks 10-12 to contribute to amniotic fluid.

questions.questionText: Which structure is a derivative of the midgut?
questions.explanation: Appendix, cecum, ascending colon are midgut derivatives.

questions.questionText: In Embryology General, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Anatomy texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Anatomy references for Embryology General. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Embryology General.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Embryology General scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Embryology General.
